{"society_power": 6.5, "state_power": 7.0, "society_change": 2.0, "state_change": -0.5, "key_event": "1988 \"No\" plebiscite and return to democratic government", "reasoning": "The \"No\" vote, competitive elections, party/union revival, and truth-seeking sharply rebuilt civil society's capacity to constrain the state, pushing Chile into the corridor. State power dips slightly as despotic autonomy is partly checked by civilian rule, though entrenched authoritarian enclaves and military autonomy keep its administrative and coercive capacity high."}
